findings.summary: Component inventory and BOM correctly extracted; -28V net misclassified as 'signal' instead of 'power'; Net tracing is broken: named nets have zero pins listed; Vacuum tubes U1/U2 (CK5654) classifi...

correct.description: All 21 components (J2, J3, J4, J5, RV1, C10–C13, R12–R21, U1, U2) are correctly identified with correct values (1uF, AudioJack2, 4K7, 470K, 200, 100K, 50K, CK5654). Component types are correctly classified. Power rails +28V and GND are identified.

incorrect.description: Audio.sch has Text GLabel labels for -28V used as a negative supply rail for R15 and R14 (the plate resistors for the vacuum tubes U1/U2), and the cathode resistors R17/R16 return to -28V as well. The net_classification in design_analysis marks '-28V' as 'signal' (net_classification["-28V"] = "signal") rather than "power". A negative supply rail that biases vacuum tube plates and cathodes should be classified as a power rail. The same misclassification occurs in TubePreAmpPower2.sch and the hierarchical TubePreAmp.sch output.

In Audio.sch output, named nets GND, -28V, and +28V all have empty 'pins' arrays (pins: []) and only a point_count. Meanwhile the capacitors C10–C13 (which are clearly wired to signal lines feeding into the tubes and to the output jacks) appear under unnamed nets (__unnamed_0 through __unnamed_7) with only single-pin membership each — e.g. __unnamed_0 has only C10 pin 1, and __unnamed_5 has only C12 pin 2 with J4 nowhere in that net. The audio signal path connectivity from J2/J3 through RV1 through C10/C11 to U1/U2 grid, and from U1/U2 plate through C12/C13 to J4/J5, is not traced — all named net membership lists for connectors and resistors are empty. This means no signal path information is usable from the per-sheet Audio.sch JSON for connectivity verification.

missed.description: U1 and U2 are CK5654 pentode vacuum tubes (footprint Valve:Valve_Noval_P, datasheet http://www.r-type.org/pdfs/5654.pdf). The analyzer classifies them as type 'ic'. There is no 'vacuum_tube' or 'valve' component type. The subcircuit analysis lists them as ICs with zero neighbor_components in Audio.sch (because no net connectivity is traced in the single-sheet parse). This misclassification affects downstream signal analysis — e.g. no amplifier or valve circuit is detected.

RV1 is a Device:R_POT_Dual (dual-gang 50K potentiometer). The analyzer classifies it as type 'resistor'. The BOM entry also places it as a resistor. A dual-gang pot is a distinct component used as a stereo volume control — the Audio.sch uses RV1 to attenuate both the left (J2) and right (J3) input channels simultaneously before the tube grid. There is no 'potentiometer' component type in the analyzer's taxonomy.

C10 and C11 are series AC-coupling capacitors placed between RV1 wiper outputs and the grids (via R13/R12 and R15/R14) of U1 and U2. C12 and C13 are output coupling capacitors from the tube plates to J4/J5 load resistors R20/R21 (100K to GND). These form classic audio high-pass coupling networks (RC high-pass with 100K and 1uF: fc ≈ 1.59 Hz, which is correct and is detected in the hierarchical run). In the single-sheet Audio.sch output, the signal_analysis.rc_filters list is empty — no RC filters are detected at all. This is because the connectivity graph is incomplete in the single-sheet parse (named nets have no pin memberships).

In Audio.sch, R13 (4K7) and R15 (470K) form a voltage divider between the -28V rail and the tube U1 grid (pin G1/CG3 via grid resistor R17). Similarly R12 (4K7) and R14 (470K) bias U2. These are grid-bias resistor networks for the pentode. The analyzer reports zero voltage_dividers in signal_analysis for the Audio.sch output, due to the connectivity tracing failure in single-sheet mode.

findings.summary: Hierarchical sheet parsing: all 4 sub-sheets loaded and all 53 components assembled correctly; Cross-sheet net connectivity correctly merged for +V, -V, +28V, -28V nets; Transistor circuits Q1/Q2 (...

correct.description: TubePreAmp.sch is the root of a 4-sheet hierarchy referencing TubePreAmpPowerInput.sch, TubePreAmpPower2.sch, and Audio.sch. The analyzer correctly parses all 4 sheets (sheets_parsed=4, sheet_files lists all four). All 53 components are present with correct values and references. The BOM across sheets aggregates correctly (9 x 470uF 35V caps, 4 x 1uF caps, 2 CK5654 tubes, etc.).

Global labels +V (output of voltage quadrupler in TubePreAmpPowerInput) are correctly connected to Q1/Q2 emitter/collector nets and R4 in TubePreAmpPower2. The +28V net in the hierarchical output correctly includes R7, C7, R19, R18 from TubePreAmpPower2 and Audio sheets. The -28V net is globally merged across sheets. The +V net in the hierarchical output lists D4 pin K, C4 pin 2, R4 pin 2, Q2 pin E — all consistent with the voltage quadrupler output driving the NPN constant-current source in TubePreAmpPower2.

The analyzer correctly identifies Q2 (D667 NPN, base via R5 1K, emitter on +V) and Q1 (D667 NPN, base via R6/R7 47Ω, collector/emitter in series) as a Darlington-like NPN constant-current source pair generating +28V. Similarly Q4 (B647 PNP, base via R9 1K, emitter on -V) and Q3 (B647 PNP, base via R10/R11 47Ω) form the negative -28V current source. Transistor type (bjt), base/collector/emitter net assignments, and base resistors are all correctly extracted.

The hierarchical output correctly detects multiple RC filters: high-pass filters C12+R20 (fc=1.59 Hz) and C13+R21 (fc=1.59 Hz) as output coupling networks; RC networks R13+C10 and R12+C11 (fc=33.86 Hz) as grid-coupling networks; low-pass filters R4+C6 (fc=0.07 Hz) as power supply ripple filters; R6+C9, R7+C9, R10+C9, R11+C9 filters on the -28V supply. Cutoff frequencies and time constants appear numerically correct.

incorrect.description: The analyzer reports two low-pass RC filters: R15 (470K) + C9 (470uF, fc≈0.00 Hz, time_constant 220.9s) and R14 (470K) + C9 (470uF). These are flagged with ground_net='GND', but C9 is the -28V output filter cap (its negative terminal goes to GND, positive to -28V). R15/R14 are tube grid-bias resistors connected from -28V to U1/U2 grid — they are not part of a filter with C9. The analyzer is hallucinating an RC filter relationship by spuriously pairing proximity of resistors and capacitors that do not form a topologically correct RC filter. Similarly R17+C9 and R16+C9 are incorrectly paired (200Ω cathode resistors for U1/U2 vs. C9 at -28V rail).

The analyzer reports an RC-network: RV1 (50K) + C11 (1uF), ground_net='__unnamed_25', cutoff=3.18 Hz, input_net='__unnamed_22', output_net='__unnamed_17'. In the schematic, RV1 is a dual-gang potentiometer whose wiper output (__unnamed_22 / __unnamed_17) connects in series through C11 (coupling cap) to R12 (4K7) and then to U2 grid. C11 is a series coupling cap, not a shunt capacitor to ground. The RV1+C11 pairing is incorrect — they do not share a topologically correct RC filter relationship. RV1 is a source impedance for the audio signal, and C11 is an AC coupling cap. The actual high-pass filter is formed by C11 with R12 (4K7) to ground (via -28V return path), not RV1.

In design_analysis.net_classification, '-28V' is classified as 'signal'. This is a negative supply rail generated by the Greinacher voltage quadrupler (D6, D7, C3, C5) and regulated by the PNP constant-current source (Q3/Q4). It powers the tubes' bias networks in Audio.sch. -28V should be classified as 'power' (or at minimum 'power_negative'). The same issue is present in Audio.sch and TubePreAmpPower2.sch individual outputs. Only GND, +V, and +28V are classified as power rails.

In the hierarchical TubePreAmp.sch output, '-V' (the raw negative output of the voltage quadrupler before the PNP regulator) is classified as 'signal'. This is an internal negative power rail that drives Q4 emitter. It should be 'power' (or 'power_internal'). The erc_warning 'no_driver' for -V (because D7 anode type='input' from pspice:DIODE) is also a false positive — the pspice:DIODE symbol uses 'input' pin type for the anode, which is an artifact of the pspice library's pin type definitions, not a real ERC violation.

In the hierarchical TubePreAmp.sch output, the subcircuits array contains 4 entries for only 2 physical ICs (U1 and U2). U1 appears once with neighbors [C1, R3, U2] (from the TubePreAmpPowerInput heater filament context) and again with neighbors [C12, R13, R15, R17, R18] (from the Audio sheet). U2 similarly appears twice. This occurs because U1 and U2 (CK5654) are multi-unit components — unit 1 is on Audio.sch, unit 2 (the heater filament, pins F1/F2/F3/F4) is on TubePreAmpPowerInput.sch. The analyzer is creating separate subcircuit entries for each unit of the same physical component rather than merging them. The two instances of U1 have different neighbor sets corresponding to their respective sheets.

The analyzer warns: 'Voltage derating margin < 50% (35.0V rated on ~28.0V rail)' for C7 (470uF 35V on +28V). The actual margin is (35-28)/35 = 20%, which is below 50% — so the warning itself is correct. However C9 (470uF 35V) is connected to -28V rail and is not flagged. The -28V rail has an estimated magnitude of 28V, and C9 is rated 35V. Since -28V is classified as 'signal', its voltage is not estimated and no derating check is applied, causing C9 to be missed. C8 (470uF 35V on -V, approximately -48V unregulated) is also not checked — -V is classified 'signal' so its voltage is unknown to the analyzer. C8 may actually be overstressed (35V rated, potentially seeing ~48V).

missed.description: TubePreAmpPowerInput.sch contains a Greinacher voltage quadrupler built from D4, D5, D6, D7 (1N4007) and C2, C3, C4, C5 (470uF 35V). The input is 12VAC from J1 (Barrel_Jack) and the output is +V (~+48V) and -V (~-48V). The schematic even has a text annotation 'Greinacher Voltage Quadrupler'. The analyzer does not detect this as a bridge_circuit, power_regulator, or any recognized power supply topology — bridge_circuits and power_regulators are both empty lists in the hierarchical output. The voltage multiplier topology (4 diodes + 4 capacitors in a quadrupler arrangement) is missed entirely.

TubePreAmpPowerInput.sch contains two LEDs (D1, D2 — Device:LED_ALT) each with a 2K2 series current-limiting resistor (R1, R2) connected to the AC input rail through D3. The schematic has a text note 'Tube LEDs'. The analyzer correctly identifies them as type 'led' in the BOM, but no LED or indicator circuit is detected in signal_analysis — buzzer_speaker_circuits is empty and there is no LED circuit detector. The LED current-limiting network (LED + series R to power rail) is a missed detection opportunity.

TubePreAmpPowerInput.sch contains tube heater connections: U1 and U2 unit 2 (CK5654 filament pins F1/F2, net __unnamed_6 and __unnamed_7), connected through R3 (2.2Ω hum-bucking resistor) and C1 (470uF 35V). The text annotation says '36 Ohm each, 6.3V each' for the tube heaters. Net __unnamed_6 correctly contains U1 pin F1, R3 pin 1, and C1 pin 2; __unnamed_7 contains U2 pin F1 and U1 pin F2. However no heater or filament circuit is detected in signal_analysis. R3 appears in no filter or special circuit — the analyzer does not recognize the heater hum-bucking topology.

findings.summary: All 16 components correctly identified with correct values and types; Capacitor C6 pin 1 incorrectly connected to GND net; C6 is the +V input filter cap; Decoupling analysis only detects C7 on +28V...

correct.description: R4, R5 (4K7, 1K NPN bias), R6, R7, R10, R11 (47Ω emitter degeneration), R8, R9 (4K7, 1K PNP bias), Q1/Q2 (D667 NPN), Q3/Q4 (B647 PNP), C6/C7/C8/C9 (470uF 35V) are all correctly extracted with correct values, references, and component type classifications (resistor, transistor, capacitor).

incorrect.description: In TubePreAmpPower2.sch, C6 (470uF 35V) is the input filter capacitor for the +V rail. From the source: C6 is a CP1 with pin 2 connected to the +V supply node and pin 1 connected to GND (GND symbol #PWR06). In the output JSON, the GND net lists 'C6 pin_number=1' and __unnamed_0 lists 'C6 pin_number=2'. The pin assignments here appear consistent with a polarized capacitor (CP1 has pin 1 as minus/GND and pin 2 as plus), so GND on pin 1 and +V on pin 2 is correct. No error here — this is correct.

The decoupling_analysis in TubePreAmpPower2.sch only reports C7 (470uF 35V) on the +28V rail. C9 (470uF 35V) is the -28V rail output filter cap and is missed because -28V is classified as 'signal'. C6 (470uF 35V) is connected to the +V input rail (unnamed net __unnamed_0) and C8 (470uF 35V) is connected to the -V input (unnamed net __unnamed_1) — neither is detected as decoupling because those rails are unnamed and not recognized as power rails. All four large electrolytic caps play a supply-filtering role in this constant-current-source circuit.

findings.summary: All 18 components correctly extracted including U1/U2 unit 2 heater instances; False ERC 'no_driver' warning on -V net due to pspice:DIODE pin type 'input'; Net connectivity for voltage quadrupler ...

correct.description: J1 (3.5mm Barrel), D1/D2 (LED), R1/R2 (2K2), R3 (2.2), D3/D4/D5/D6/D7 (1N4007), C1/C2/C3/C4/C5 (470uF 35V), U1/U2 unit 2 (CK5654 heater) are all correctly identified with the right values and references. The unit=2 field on U1/U2 correctly marks these as the heater unit of the multi-unit CK5654 symbol.

The nets for the Greinacher quadrupler are correctly traced: __unnamed_0 (J1 pin 2, D3 anode, C2 pin 2, C3 pin 1) is the AC input node; __unnamed_2 (C2 pin 1, D4 anode, D5 cathode) and __unnamed_3 (C3 pin 2, D6 anode, D7 cathode) are the intermediate doubler nodes; +V (D4 cathode, C4 pin 2) is the positive output; -V (D7 anode, C5 pin 1) is the negative output. This cross-cap diode connectivity is correctly resolved.

incorrect.description: The analyzer emits an ERC warning: 'Net -V has input pins but no output driver' citing D7 pin 2 (A, type=input) and C5 pin 1 (passive). This is a false positive: the pspice:DIODE symbol defines its anode (A) as pin type 'input', which is a KiCad library convention for pspice-derived symbols, not a real input signal pin. In reality D7 anode is a power output terminal — it is the cathode of the last stage in the Greinacher quadrupler and drives the -V power rail through C5. The warning should not fire for pspice:DIODE anode pins.

missed.description: D3 (1N4007) is placed in series with the AC input line between J1 (Barrel_Jack pin 2) and the voltage quadrupler / LED circuit. It functions as a half-wave rectifier and reverse-polarity protection for the LED indicators. The protection_devices list in signal_analysis is empty — D3 is not recognized as a protection or rectifier device.
